description: Create canonical shared fixtures and parity tests proving Web and Ink consume the same terminal rows, cards, ordering metadata, streaming state, details, tones, and redaction behavior.

userStories.title: Build canonical compact-terminal fixture helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want one fixture source for all major terminal row kinds so Web and Ink tests cannot drift silently.
acceptanceCriteria: Add canonical fixture helpers for user message, assistant message, running assistant partial, reasoning/thinking, tool call, tool result, exploring/tool group, yielded run, compaction, execution command, status command/result, thinking/model/login menu rows, error row, and expandable details
Fixtures include realistic order metadata: orderSource, orderStreamId, orderStreamFrameIndex, event id, run id, trace node id where applicable, and stable local command ids for CLI-only rows
At least one fixture is derived from or shaped like a real event-log/runtime payload, not only hand-written display text
Fixtures include secret-bearing values, unavailable values, zero values, high-usage values, long markdown, long JSON, and narrow-width labels
Fixtures live in a neutral test/helper location and do not import Web DOM or Ink components
Focused fixture/model tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Assert shared row order, descriptors, tones, and redaction
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want model tests that prove both renderers start from identical terminal semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared tests assert exact CompactTerminalRow kind/status sequence for normal transcript and streaming transcript fixtures
Shared tests assert buildTerminalCardDescriptor output for status, thinking, model, login, tool, yielded-run, compaction, command, and error rows
Tests assert token tones and prefix-glyph semantics for running, done, error, reasoning, command, detail, and prompt-like rows
Tests assert context/provider progress descriptors preserve unavailable, zero, warning, high, label, percentage, and raw count semantics
Tests assert secret redaction happens in shared descriptors before Web or Ink rendering
Focused shared-model tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Preserve streaming and chronological ordering semantics
userStories.description: As a user, I want live updates, command results, and tool rows to stay in the same order across Web and TUI.
acceptanceCriteria: Streaming fixture keeps running assistant rows after prior user/tool rows and before later command/status rows
Running tool calls use running/action semantics and keep orderStream metadata visible to renderer tests or semantic hooks
Command result rows appended during streaming appear after the current transcript tail without reordering older rows
Web sticky-follow assumptions and Ink tail-window assumptions are covered by semantic tests or documented source checks
Tests include dedupe/stable id expectations for local command rows where persisted events may later echo them
Focused streaming/order tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Share details and bounded previews across Web and Ink
userStories.description: As a user, I want details to expose the same input/output/error information without unbounded dumps.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared detail fixture exposes Input, Output, Error, Linked session, command args, tool result preview, and large JSON/markdown detail items
Web and Ink tests assert detail labels are equivalent and collapsed previews preserve error/status meaning
Large JSON, long markdown, and long tool output are bounded before or during renderer output according to shared/renderer responsibilities
No detail fixture expands full secret-bearing payloads by default; redacted values are asserted in preview and details
Focused detail/bounded-output tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Prove Web and Ink consume the same fixture source
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want Web and Ink tests tied to the same fixtures so renderer drift fails early.
acceptanceCriteria: Web Compact Terminal tests or source-level checks import or derive from the canonical fixture source
Ink renderer tests import or derive from the canonical fixture source
Web rendered/source output exposes stable semantic hooks for row kind, status, trace node id, event id, run id, order metadata, and shared card kind where feasible
Ink output from the same fixture contains equivalent labels, markers, row order, statuses, and redacted text
Web-impacting checks include npm run chat-ui:typecheck and npm run chat-ui:build where changed files require them
Focused Web/Ink parity fixture tests pass
Full npm test passes before this PRD is marked complete
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes
